title: Fermented Slovakian Cottage Cheese Curd
description: A traditional fermented Slovakian dish made with cottage cheese and minimal ingredients, suitable for a solo dinner. Prepared in a copper mug, this recipe is also vegan-friendly and suitable for a Buddhist temple.
instructions: Whisk together cottage cheese and water in a copper mug until smooth.
Add fermented apple cider vinegar and salt, whisk until combined.
Cover the mug with a cloth or plastic wrap and let it rest at room temperature for 3-4 hours, or until slightly thickened and bubbly.
Garnish with fresh dill, if desired.
Serve the fermented cottage cheese curd as a solo dinner, or store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
If desired, serve with a side of pickled vegetables or a simple salad.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is left to ferment for several days, but a 3-4 hour fermentation time is recommended for a milder flavor.
